title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCXCII. Lullabies.
content:
Bye, O my baby!
                When I was a lady,
        O then my poor baby did'nt cry!
                But my baby is weeping,
                For want of good keeping,
        Oh, I fear my poor baby will die!
meaning:
"Nursery Rhyme. CCCXCII. Lullabies." is a quintessential example of Unknown's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
